rationale: V2.1 is the clearest upstream layer because it focuses on observed mine-production concentration and has the strongest existing visual explanation potential.
reader_value: Shows where mine production is concentrated before processing/refining or downstream manufacturing.
improvement_path: Later visual and interpretive polish is useful, but only after public-boundary review.
blocked_interpretation: Do not interpret upstream concentration as supply-chain vulnerability, investment signal, geopolitical ranking, forecast, or validated risk score.

primary_artifact_or_line: V2.4-V2.11 downstream concentration and traceability line
rationale: Although earlier wording sometimes used downstream, the strongest current evidence line concerns processing/refining concentration and traceability, which is better described as midstream.
reader_value: Explains how concentration can change between mining and processing/refining, with lithium source-traceable, manganese proxy-limited, and other minerals still public-snapshot.
improvement_path: Continue with Natural Graphite Product-Definition Traceability if source enrichment continues.
blocked_interpretation: Do not claim all minerals have equivalent source strength or that processing/refining concentration proves bottlenecks, vulnerability, disruption probability, or risk ranking.

rationale: V2.2 has interpretive value as the first mining-versus-processing/refining bridge, but V2.4-V2.11 now provide the cleaner traceability-controlled version.
reader_value: Can help explain the evolution from upstream-only concentration to processing/refining comparison.
improvement_path: Do not expand V2.2 as a main analysis line; cross-reference or supersede with V2.4-V2.11 where appropriate.
blocked_interpretation: Do not use V2.2 as the controlling downstream evidence layer.

primary_artifact_or_line: V2.3 cobalt and nickel directional evidence
rationale: V2.3 is evidence-limited and directional. It does not currently form a strong standalone downstream layer.
reader_value: Can remain as historical evidence-boundary context or be folded into a legacy appendix.
improvement_path: Supersede or integrate rather than polish as a main reader-facing stage.
blocked_interpretation: Do not present V2.3 as completed downstream bottleneck, manufacturing, demand, or supply-chain resilience analysis.

rationale: True downstream should refer to battery/cell manufacturing, cathode/anode material production, EV battery demand, regional demand exposure, or end-market dependency rather than mining-versus-refining concentration alone.
reader_value: Would create a fuller value-chain story if reliable raw data can be sourced.
improvement_path: Start only after source feasibility confirms strong public data for downstream manufacturing or demand indicators.
blocked_interpretation: Do not imply the current project already has a full downstream layer.
